Niche Selection
Module 2
Markets are Very Different…	Markets are Very Different…	Markets are Very Different…	Markets are Very Different…
• They have different characteristics
• This lesson will help you identify a niche to start
• I started in the Stock Market Niche (investing and
trading)
• Moved to teaching about Building a Business
Online
• Your first niche will not necessarily be your last
Where to Start	Where to Start	Where to Start	Where to Start
• Do you want to follow your passion?
• Do you want to follow the money (regardless of
your interest in that market)?
• Which ever you choose is okay
• There’s no right or wrong method

Let’s Brainstorm…	Let’s Brainstorm…	Let’s Brainstorm…	Let’s Brainstorm…
Approach #1	Approach #1	Approach #1	Approach #1 –––– Focus on You	Focus on You	Focus on You	Focus on You
• What are your core passions and interests?
• What skills have you developed?
• What knowledge have you acquired?
• What challenges have you overcome?
• What would other people say you do really well?
What do people ask you for advice about?
My Challenge to Identify a Niche…	My Challenge to Identify a Niche…	My Challenge to Identify a Niche…	My Challenge to Identify a Niche…
• When I first started out learning about how to
create info products…
• I was studying the stock market (my passion)
• Full day as a stay at home dad
• Free time was spent studying the stock market
• Would lay in bed at night trying to figure out what I
could publish about…
• Lightbulb moment – the answer is often right under
your nose
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 5
Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2 –––– Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar
• General idea of what direction you might go in…
• Think about who your perfect client would be (aka
your avatar)
Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2 –––– Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar
• What do people to come to you for help with or ask
you questions about?
• What do your existing clients or prospects struggle
with?
• What pain points, challenges, or common
questions are people coming to you with?
Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2 –––– Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar
• What common questions are people asking on
blogs, or forums?
• What are the best-selling books on your topic?
• Go to Amazon and type in the keywords on your
topic and find top ranking books
• Go to google and type in your topic keyword +
“forums”
• Find the sticky topics at the top of forums or most
popular topics
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 17 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 6
Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2	Approach #2 –––– Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar	Focus on Your Avatar
• If you have a list, ask them what they’re struggling
with…
• “If you could sit down with me for coffee, what
would be the number one question you would
ask me?”
Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3 –––– Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money
• Find out who is making money and see how you
can do it differently or do it better
• Can you bring your own voice or personality to it?
• Look at who’s advertising in your market
• Look at magazines on your topic – find ads that are
running consistently (tells you it works)
Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3 –––– Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money
• Evergreen markets (vs. seasonal or fad)
• You want a market that’s been around – that
continues to be around
• Don’t build your business on a fad (scrabble tiles)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 17 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 7
Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3 –––– Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money
• If you build your business around a holiday find
something that offsets the rest of the year
• Seasonal – like skiing in the fall/winter and
mountain biking in the spring/summer – they offset
each other
Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3	Approach #3 –––– Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money	Follow the Money
• Look for markets with multiple types of products at
all price points
• Go to Clickbank.com and search the marketplace
by niche topics
• Find a product on clickbank.com that is doing
really well in a niche that hasn’t been exposed to
product launches and focus there
• Focus in the “Big 4” proven markets and combine
them or niche down

Research and Test…	Research and Test…	Research and Test…	Research and Test…
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Google your niche and see what sites come up…
• Look for advertisers
• Who’s at the top of the natural search (not
sponsored results)?
• What products they’re selling?
• How are they building a list?
• What is their lead magnet?
• Subscribe to the top advertisers and top natural
search results
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 9
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Create a spreadsheet
• List in order of search appearance
• Name
• URL
• Building a list or not
• What they’re selling
• Price points
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Look at their emails
• What are they selling?
• Who’s products are they promoting?
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Are people selling in these markets?
• If there is NO competition, then be very careful
• Find a successful market and be better/unique
• Find a market that people are passionate about
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 10
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Find rabid markets
• Golf
• Scuba Diving
• Dog Training – Pets in General
• Relationships
• How to Build a Business
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Find markets people are super passionate about
• Look for rabid markets vs. non-rabid
• Selling tires…
• Race tires for rally cars – people are into that
• General tires for everyday cars… people aren’t
excited about that
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Look at popular magazines to get ideas
• You’ll start to see niches you wouldn’t dream of
• Guitar
• Playing electric guitars
• Acoustic guitars
• Guitar collectors
• Remodeling guitars...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 11
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Go to amazon.com and search for hot markets
• Is there a trade association for that market?
• Is there a high “cost per click” (CPC – $4-5 per click
is awesome)?
• Google Adwords (adwords.google.com - now
ads.google.com)
• Free to join
• See how much people are paying for clicks
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Go back to your spreadsheet…
• Join their affiliate program and observe
• Buy some advertising and send traffic to that
program and see how well it converts
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Create a list of possible offers you could make and
analyze in terms of…
• Demand
• Competition
• Quality
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 17 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 12
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Consider buying their programs and watching their
sales process
• What are they doing, how are they doing it?
• What are the hot points?
• What upsells, downsells are they offering?
• How high quality is their marketing and their
product?
• How strong is their Prelaunch Content?
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• What do you have to do to compete?
• What’s going to be your speed to market?
• It’s not so much about competition as
cooperation…
• Do people promote for each other as Joint
Venture partners?
• You promote for them, generally they’ll promote
for you
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Main factors for demand:
• Search volume
• Google Adwords has a keyword tool that tells
you about search volume
• You typically want at least 100k searches for all
main keywords
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 17 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 13
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Are people paying for advertisements?
• What kind of competition is there?
• Are people in that marketing buying online?
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Write the sales letter, even better, a squeeze page
before the product even exists and send traffic to it
• PPC traffic is quickest
• Look for good conversions (20%)
Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• This is something I did when I was changing
niches…
• I put up an opt-in page in March 2003
• “Put in your email address and I’ll share
everything I know about building a business
online”
• I didn’t start selling anything until October 2005
• My first instinct was to start building a list
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test	Research and Test
• Once you’ve found your idea/niche, start building
your list and building a relationship with your list
• You can do that before you’re even sure to gauge
success in that market at getting people to opt-in
What to Avoid…	What to Avoid…	What to Avoid…	What to Avoid…
Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert
• You don’t have to be the expert
• Leverage the knowledge of those who are experts
• There are a ton of people who would love to be
interviewed
• Look at all the podcasts… seems like most are
people interviewing each other
• Share what you learn from other experts, giving
credit
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 17 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 15
Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert
• In any market, most people are beginners
• Everyone starts as a beginner
• You only have to be a few steps ahead to pull them
forward
• In the land of the blind, the one-eyed man is king!
Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert
• On the flip side, you don’t want to do anything or
teach anything that would put people in harm
because you’re “faking it”
• Example: Teaching something where someone
could lose money
• When I was offering trading advice I had research
experience AND a mentor that was an expert
• Be careful that you have the experience, expertise,
or you bring in other expertise/mentors
Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert
• Can you help them…
• Take action?
• Stay accountable?
• Produce real results?
• (This is more important than your status as an
expert)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert	Thinking… I am Not Enough of an Expert
• Great example – John Gallagher
• Teaches about edible, medicinal plants and
herbs
• Been studying for years and years
• He brings experts to his market
Following a Passion Without Testing the	Following a Passion Without Testing the	Following a Passion Without Testing the	Following a Passion Without Testing the
Market	Market	Market	Market
• Offering what you think the market wants and not
what they actually want
• Offering what you think the market
needs
	needs
	needs
	needs and not
actually what they want
• It’s easier to sell the cure than to sell prevention
• Example – in Guitar – selling the need – the core
fundamental: How to practice correctly, instead of
what people want
• Sell them what they want, give them what they
need
Following a Passion Without Testing the	Following a Passion Without Testing the	Following a Passion Without Testing the	Following a Passion Without Testing the
Market	Market	Market	Market
• Do your research
• Get feedback in the development of your product
or service

• Trying to serve everyone
